title: Upon Lusk.
content:
In Den'shire Kersey Lusk, when he was dead,
    Would shrouded be and therewith buried.
    When his assigns asked him the reason why,
    He said, because he got his wealth thereby.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Lusk.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
